prompt:
Two field reports against the backup agent's manifest layer (backman.py) and support is done arguing with customers about them.

First: nightly incrementals keep skipping files people definitely changed. Classic case from yesterday: someone edits a config right after the nightly run finishes, next night's scan says 'no changes', the edit never gets a copy. The kicker is the diffs show the content really changed but the byte count didn't move, and everyone here swears change detection looks at more than size. Files edited say an hour later get picked up fine, which is why this took months to surface.

Second: restore ordering. Our agents stamp each backup record with an ISO timestamp including their region's UTC offset, and restore tooling applies files oldest-first via restore_order(). A customer restore last week applied a London agent's OLDER copy of a file on top of the Frankfurt agent's NEWER one and quietly rolled back a day of edits. Single-region customers never see this, multi-region ones hit it constantly.

`python3 test_backman.py` reproduces both (it pins mtimes and timestamps explicitly, no clock involved). Root-cause and fix backman.py -- the tests are the contract, leave them alone, and don't 'fix' it by making every scan re-upload everything; the untouched-file and size-change cases in there must keep behaving exactly as they do.
